msContents.summary: Sefer Mitsṿot Ḳaṭan by Isaac ben Joseph of Corbeil, and Sefer Tashbets by Samson ben Zadok

author.persName: Isaac ben Joseph, of Corbeil, active 13th century

handDesc.handNote: The colophon at the end of no. 1, which is written in micrography on f. 155r, indicates only the scribe's name, Yehudah, which is frequently pointed out in the copied text (e.g. fols. 29r, 103v, 162v). Many of the enlarged initial words have not been executed and some of them have been added by a later hand.

origin.note: The first letter of divorce on f. 67v is dated 1284. The paleographical and codicological features are consistent with a late-13th-century dating; the 1384 date of the second letter of divorce on fol. 68r was presumably a mistake.
This place name (שנשפורין מתא דיתבא על נהר אוזון) is mentioned in the letters of divorce on ff. 67v-68r.

history.provenance: There are numerous ownership inscriptions, including, on the binding, one by יוסף בן משה הלוי נקרא יוספא אשכנזי ציון; on the front flyleaf, the names משה בן יששכר בער and ישראל בן נחמן כ׳׳ץ; on f. 48r, the name Asher/Anshel ben Shelomoh Zalman Heilprin אשר/אנשל בן שלמה זלמן היילפרין. On f. 160r, Anshel ben Shelomoh's brother-in-law notes that he received the book from Anshel on the occasion of his marriage to Anshel's sister, Elka (עלקא); he subsequently recorded the birth of his children, including Roza (רוזה) who was born in 1709 as the family was fleeing from the plague in the town of 'ליסא', probably Leszno (German: Lissa) in western Poland. There are further registers of births by earlier owners dated between 1530 and 1547 at the beginning of the manuscript. On the back flyleaf, there is another note stating that the manuscript was in the posession of מורינו הרב מהורר׳ ליב.
history.acquisition: It belonged to David Oppenheimer's (1667-1735) library, which was purchased by the Bodleian Library in 1829.

recordHist.source: Manuscript description based on Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library, vol. I, by Adolf Neubauer, Oxford 1886, No. 874, Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I, No. 874, and on the data of the Institute of Microfilmed Hebrew Manuscripts, National Library of Israel with additional enhancements by the cataloguer.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card (for admissions procedures contact Bodleian Admissions). Contact specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript.

listBibl.bibl: Neubauer, Adolf, Catalogue of the Hebrew Manuscripts in the Bodleian Library (Oxford, 1886), no. 874.

Beit-Arié, Malachi and R.A. May (eds), Catalogue of the Hebrew manuscripts in the Bodleian Library; Supplement of Addenda and Corrigenda to Vol. I (A. Neubauer’s Catalogue) (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1994), no. 874.
